description: A delete-heavy or queue-like access pattern accumulates tombstones that make reads slow or fail.
body: Failure mode: modelling a queue or a frequently-deleted dataset on one partition leaves thousands of tombstones that every read must scan, so latency climbs and eventually a read crosses the tombstone failure threshold and errors out. Detection signals: query tracing reports a high tombstones-scanned count, reads warn about tombstone thresholds in the log, and latency correlates with delete volume. Do not build queues or high-churn delete patterns on a single Cassandra partition. Safer fallback: redesign to append-only with TimeWindowCompactionStrategy and TTL so whole SSTables expire, or move the queue workload to a purpose-built system, and confirm tombstone scans drop.
